title: alsine tenuifolia var. hybrida
intro: Alsine tenuifolia var. hybrida, also known as creeping saxifrage or small saxifrage, is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and rounded leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its ability to promote digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and diarrhea. Alsine tenuifolia var. hybrida is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its rich content of antioxidants and flavonoids. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
